Northampton Tree Planting and Removal 2015-2025

Author

Kent S Johnson

Introduction

This report summarizes tree plantings and subsequent removals for the City of Northampton in the years 2015 to 2025.

The analysis is based on tree data from the City Tree Warden.

Summary

In this period, 2,500 trees were planted. Of these, 2,365 (95%) have survived to date and 135 (5%) have been removed.

Yearly planting and removal

This table and chart show planting and removals by planting year. They count removals against the planting year; the removal numbers for recent years may increase over time as more trees are removed.

Change in top species

The percentages of most of the top species in the urban forest have declined as other species have been planted. Of the top ten species, only Gleditsia triacanthos shows a small increase in percentage.

A general guideline is that an urban forest should contain no more than 10% of any one species. In the 2015 survey, Acer rubrum nearly reached this value, but planting in other families has decreased the percentage of Acer rubrum below 10%.

Change in top genera

The percentages of several genera in the urban forest have declined as other genera have been planted. The percentage of Quercus has only declined slightly because many were planted. Tilia shows the greatest increase in percentage.

A general guideline is that an urban forest should contain no more than 20% of any one genus. Acer is the only genus in Northampton that exceeds this value.

Top genera as percent of total urban forest
Top ten genera overall

2025

Change in top families

The percentages of families Sapindaceae and Pinaceae in the urban forest have declined as other families have been planted. The percentage of Fagaceae has not changed much because many were planted. The percentages of Rosaceae, Fabaceae and Malvaceae have increased due to significant numbers of new plantings.

A general guideline is that an urban forest should contain no more than 30% of any one family. In the 2015 survey, Sapindaceae exceeded this value, but planting in other families has decreased the percentage of Sapindaceae below 30%.

Top families as percent of total urban forest
Top ten families overall

Planting in Environmental Justice communities

Northampton includes seven 2020 Census block groups which are classified as Environmental Justice populations - four with substantial minority populations and three with both minority and low-income populations.

This table shows the number of trees planted each year in Environmental Justice communities of Northampton.

Trees planted in Environmental Justice communities
Year Count
2015 39
2016 22
2017 80
2018 64
2019 92
2020 31
2021 147
2022 108
2023 58
2024 38
2025 28
Total 707
Data: Northampton Tree Warden | Analysis: Kent S Johnson

Planting location description

The trees planted are predominantly in the tree belt and right-of-way (67%) and set back (21%) with 12% in other locations.

Planting map

This map shows the approximate locations of the 2,365 surviving trees. Overlays show Northampton wards and Environmental Justice (EJ) communities.
